decisions.candidate_summary: Add memory entry to flag single-vendor dependency risks in analyses when user monitors Anthropic platform reliability.

reasoning.signal_assessment: Genuine signal from multiple documented incidents (org bans, degradations, billing anomalies). Not pipeline noise. However, the signal has already been actioned.
reasoning.memory_consistency: Functionally duplicates existing entry 2046ed2f: 'Claude will proactively surface vendor concentration risk during due diligence without prompting.' Same behavior, different words.
reasoning.eval_alignment: No eval data. Base entry applied same day, unvalidated. Cannot assess whether existing entry is sufficient.
reasoning.risk_assessment: Low risk either way. Applying creates redundancy; skipping relies on existing entry. Prefer minimal memory footprint until eval validates current state.

hypothesis.prediction: Skipping this candidate will not reduce vendor risk flagging behavior because existing entry 2046ed2f already provides the same instruction.
hypothesis.falsification_condition: If vendor risk is not surfaced in due diligence contexts where it is material, and user feedback indicates the existing entry is insufficient, this skip was wrong.

decisions.candidate_summary: Default to flagging which model version produced a response when version matters for quality.

reasoning.signal_assessment: Real signal about Opus 4.6 vs 4.7 quality divergence. Users document significant differences in hallucination, verbosity, instruction-following.
reasoning.memory_consistency: Covered by combination of entries b6da3b03 (name model version when quality degrades) and e5fb8d38 (surface model and reasoning mode on complex high-stakes sessions). This candidate adds marginal specificity.
reasoning.eval_alignment: No eval data on model version awareness behavior from existing entries.
reasoning.risk_assessment: Low. Instruction creep risk — adding a third overlapping entry. Prefer to validate existing entries first.

hypothesis.prediction: Existing entries b6da3b03 and e5fb8d38 will produce sufficient model version awareness without this additional instruction.
hypothesis.expected_magnitude: 0% incremental improvement vs applying this candidate
hypothesis.falsification_condition: If users are surprised by model version switches affecting output quality despite existing entries, the skip was wrong.

decisions.candidate_summary: Add Anthropic-specific vendor risk factors (degradations, subscription changes, suspensions, Mythos) to due diligence framework.

reasoning.signal_assessment: Strong signal. Documented patterns are real and multiple. The proposal to create an Anthropic-specific risk library is grounded in evidence.
reasoning.memory_consistency: Extends existing entry 2046ed2f (vendor concentration risk). Not a contradiction — an elaboration. However, encoding vendor-specific failure catalogs is a different category than general capability instructions.
reasoning.eval_alignment: No eval data on whether general vendor-risk entry surfaces Anthropic-specific concerns when material.
reasoning.risk_assessment: Medium. Anthropic critiquing Anthropic in user documents creates maintenance burden and potential awkwardness. More durable to let general framework handle specific cases.

hypothesis.prediction: The general vendor concentration risk entry (2046ed2f) will surface Anthropic-specific concerns when they are material to due diligence, making this explicit catalog unnecessary.
hypothesis.expected_magnitude: General entry sufficient for 80%+ of cases
hypothesis.falsification_condition: If user conducts Anthropic vendor assessment and general entry fails to surface documented risk factors, apply this candidate.

decisions.defer_condition: Apply if eval shows general vendor-risk entry doesn't surface Anthropic-specific concerns when material, OR if user explicitly requests this specificity.

decisions.candidate_summary: Proactively surface data residency, compliance posture, and audit trail gaps in AI vendor documentation.

reasoning.signal_assessment: Valid signal from HN about regulated-industry AI evaluation struggles. The gap is real.
reasoning.memory_consistency: Directly duplicates existing entry 8ea8cce2: 'Claude will include compliance and data residency as default first-order criteria in vendor evaluations without prompting.' Same behavior.
reasoning.eval_alignment: No eval data. Entry applied same day.

hypothesis.prediction: Skipping creates no behavior gap; existing entry 8ea8cce2 provides identical functionality.
hypothesis.falsification_condition: If compliance and data residency are not surfaced in vendor evaluations despite entry 8ea8cce2, revisit.

decisions.candidate_summary: Apply same competing-frameworks standard to self-referential AI vendor assessments; include candid Anthropic reliability acknowledgment.

reasoning.signal_assessment: Valid concern about provider sycophancy in self-referential contexts. The principle is sound.
reasoning.memory_consistency: Philosophically consistent with profile's research-grade depth and competing frameworks. The specific instruction (explicit Anthropic critique) is an implementation that could overcorrect.
reasoning.eval_alignment: No data on self-referential vendor analysis quality. No baseline for sycophancy detection.
reasoning.risk_assessment: Medium. Explicit instruction to critique own provider could produce forced negativity. Prefer to observe natural behavior first.

hypothesis.prediction: Existing research-grade depth instructions will produce balanced self-referential analysis without special-case handling for Anthropic.
hypothesis.falsification_condition: If Claude systematically avoids Anthropic criticism in vendor contexts despite documented issues, apply this candidate.

decisions.defer_condition: Apply if user observes Claude systematically avoiding Anthropic criticism in vendor contexts despite documented issues.

decisions.candidate_summary: Excel/spreadsheet outputs should proactively flag formula inconsistencies, methodology risks, and data integrity issues; default to adversarial audit posture.

reasoning.signal_assessment: Good signal documenting Claude's strength in catching formula errors. Failure mode identified: clean output without explaining what was wrong.
reasoning.memory_consistency: No existing entry covers spreadsheet-specific behavior. Philosophically consistent with profile's accuracy preference and OUTPUT VERIFICATION section.
reasoning.eval_alignment: No data on spreadsheet task quality, but profile's 'comfort for accuracy' trade supports this direction.
reasoning.risk_assessment: Low. Bounded capability instruction. Worst case: over-flagging minor issues, which is acceptable given profile's accuracy preference. Reversible.

hypothesis.prediction: Adding this instruction will increase the rate at which Claude flags formula and data integrity issues in spreadsheet outputs, improving accuracy for documents entering formal review.
hypothesis.falsification_condition: If spreadsheet outputs continue to present clean results without flagging issues that are later caught in review, the instruction is not producing the intended behavior.

decisions.candidate_summary: Add adversarial self-audit mode/instruction for complex multi-step tasks; audit pass before returning output.

reasoning.signal_assessment: Good signal. Practitioners report explicit 'audit your work' instructions materially improve Opus 4.7 output on complex tasks.
reasoning.memory_consistency: Distinct from OUTPUT VERIFICATION (citation uncertainty) and DRIFT MODE (context saturation). This addresses error-catching in multi-step work. Extends existing mode infrastructure without redundancy.
reasoning.eval_alignment: No data on audit pass effectiveness, but the practitioner signal is consistent.
reasoning.risk_assessment: Low-medium. Could add token overhead for audits that don't catch errors. Profile already establishes mode infrastructure, so architectural fit is good.

hypothesis.prediction: Adding adversarial self-audit mode will reduce plausible-looking errors in multi-step task outputs by catching them before delivery.
hypothesis.expected_magnitude: -20% error rate on complex multi-step tasks
hypothesis.falsification_condition: If error rates on multi-step tasks do not decrease, or if audit overhead significantly increases session token usage without corresponding quality improvement, the instruction is not net positive.

decisions.candidate_summary: Handle context degradation by surfacing proactive flag before next substantive output.

reasoning.signal_assessment: Good signal about silent mid-session degradation. Real problem.
reasoning.memory_consistency: Directly duplicates DRIFT MODE (entry 8cb7fffc): 'automatic context saturation detection with structured handoff note.' Same behavior with different phrasing.
reasoning.eval_alignment: No eval data. DRIFT MODE applied same day, unvalidated.

hypothesis.prediction: DRIFT MODE already provides context degradation flagging; skipping this candidate creates no behavior gap.
hypothesis.falsification_condition: If context degradation is not flagged proactively despite DRIFT MODE, revisit.

decisions.candidate_summary: hallucination_guard skill — epistemic discipline on factual claims with three confidence tiers, no fabrication rule, correction acknowledgment protocol.

reasoning.signal_assessment: Good signal about fabricated citations/URLs and defensive behavior when challenged. Skill skeleton is well-structured.
reasoning.memory_consistency: Extends OUTPUT VERIFICATION (hallucination self-audit with [verify] markers) into a comprehensive skill with concrete behaviors. Refinement, not contradiction.
reasoning.eval_alignment: No data on current hallucination rates, but OUTPUT VERIFICATION was applied same day on same concern.
reasoning.risk_assessment: Low-medium. Risk of over-cautious flagging. Profile's accuracy preference suggests acceptable trade. Skill format allows toggling if overcorrection occurs.

hypothesis.prediction: hallucination_guard skill will reduce fabricated citations and defensive behavior when challenged, improving document accuracy for formal review.
hypothesis.expected_magnitude: +25% reduction in fabricated or unverifiable citations
hypothesis.falsification_condition: If fabricated citations persist at baseline rate, or if over-flagging significantly reduces output utility, the skill is not producing net value.

decisions.candidate_summary: Profile addition — explicit confidence tiering for factual claims, never fabricate sources, correction acknowledgment.

reasoning.signal_assessment: Same signal as hallucination_guard skill (candidate 9). Same source posts.
reasoning.memory_consistency: Overlaps with OUTPUT VERIFICATION and candidate 9 (hallucination_guard skill). If candidate 9 is applied, this is redundant.
reasoning.risk_assessment: Low. Dependency relationship resolved by preferring skill format.

hypothesis.prediction: hallucination_guard skill (candidate 9) will provide the same behavior as this profile addition; applying both is redundant.
hypothesis.falsification_condition: If hallucination_guard skill does not produce expected behavior and this profile addition would have, the skip was wrong.

decisions.candidate_summary: Technique — ask for framework instead of direct answer to shift AI from answer-generator to thinking-scaffold.

reasoning.signal_assessment: Good signal from practitioner posts. The technique is sound: framework requests produce more durable outputs.
reasoning.memory_consistency: User-behavior technique, not Claude configuration. No conflict with memory state.
reasoning.risk_assessment: Very low. Advisory content for user. Worst case: technique doesn't help user's specific workflow.

hypothesis.prediction: Surfacing this technique will give user a new prompting strategy that produces more useful outputs on complex decisions.
hypothesis.expected_magnitude: User tries technique at least once
hypothesis.falsification_condition: If user reports the technique produced worse outputs than direct questions, the recommendation was unhelpful.

decisions.candidate_summary: Technique — maintain portable context profile file to paste into new sessions across AI tools.

reasoning.signal_assessment: Good signal. Practical technique for cross-platform context continuity. Directly relevant given CLAIRE's context management approach.

hypothesis.prediction: Surfacing this technique will help user maintain context continuity across multiple AI tools.
hypothesis.falsification_condition: If user reports the technique adds overhead without improving response quality across tools, recommendation was unhelpful.

decisions.candidate_summary: Technique — route different task types to different AI models based on relative strengths.

reasoning.signal_assessment: Good signal. Practical workflow optimization for multi-tool users.

hypothesis.prediction: Surfacing this technique will help user optimize task routing across multiple AI tools.
hypothesis.expected_magnitude: User explicitly routes task categories to different models
hypothesis.falsification_condition: If user reports multi-model routing increases overhead without quality benefit, recommendation was unhelpful.

decisions.candidate_summary: Flag model-version-related quality risks in analytical document metadata.

reasoning.signal_assessment: Medium signal (0.63). Documents Opus 4.7 regressions. The proposal to embed version risk flags in output documents is more invasive than awareness instructions.
reasoning.memory_consistency: Extends entries about model version awareness. Not a contradiction but a significant step further — putting caveats in user documents.
reasoning.eval_alignment: No data on whether current entries produce sufficient version awareness. Need baseline.
reasoning.risk_assessment: Medium. Document-level version disclaimers could be useful (transparency) or annoying (unnecessary caveats). Source confidence is MEDIUM.

hypothesis.prediction: Existing model version awareness entries will surface version concerns conversationally; document-level flags add marginal value.
hypothesis.falsification_condition: If users are surprised by version-related quality issues in documents that should have been flagged, apply this candidate.

decisions.defer_condition: Apply if eval shows users are surprised by version-related quality issues that weren't flagged, OR if user explicitly requests document-level version metadata.

decisions.candidate_summary: Project files should use Markdown format; avoid large monolithic context blobs.

reasoning.signal_assessment: Medium signal (0.61). Posts suggest markdown outperforms PDF, chunked outperforms monolithic.
reasoning.memory_consistency: Existing entry 76b2a82b covers the core principle: 'recommend chunked structure over monolithic files.' This candidate adds Markdown format preference specifically.
reasoning.risk_assessment: Low. The Markdown preference is reasonable but more opinionated than existing entry. User may prefer other formats.

hypothesis.prediction: Existing 'chunked over monolithic' entry covers the core principle; Markdown-specific preference is implementation detail that may not generalize.
hypothesis.falsification_condition: If user reports that Claude recommends monolithic or non-Markdown approaches when Markdown would have been clearly better, revisit.

decision_record.session_notes: First decision cycle with no eval history — all confidence calibration is necessarily loose. High redundancy observed: 5 candidates duplicated same-day memory entries, suggesting digest pipeline needs stronger memory_state filtering. Three technique candidates (user-behavior) are categorically different from profile/memory changes and should route to separate output stream. Two candidates (b47505727888b176, 993e10529720f2e2) propose explicit Anthropic self-critique — deferred pending observation of whether existing entries produce sycophancy bias. Applied: spreadsheet audit posture (fills genuine gap), adversarial self-audit mode (extends existing mode infrastructure), hallucination_guard skill (operationalizes OUTPUT VERIFICATION philosophy), and three user-behavior techniques (low-cost, sound advice). Key dependency: hallucination_guard skill supersedes profile addition candidate with same intent. Recommend adding 'summary' field to change_log memory_edit entries per _meta note.
